title: Links

No new artifact paths/fields/GUIDs/decoders/anomaly thresholds/detection predicates documented in THIS post. Carvey's "don't take artifact at face value, read research before claiming program execution from ShimCache/AmCache" is process guidance, not an encodable predicate (no boundary, no observable). 0 artifacts/0 heuristics.

title: Devices
notes: re-reviewed under full standard (artifacts + heuristics): post is largely meta-discussion on analysis goals and playbook discipline. The single new factual nugget is that Microsoft-Windows-DriverFrameworks-UserMode/Operational is now disabled by default on modern Windows and "doesn't provide much depth" even when enabled; analysts arriving post-incident find it empty. USBSTOR, EMDMgmt, setupapi.dev.log, MsiInstaller, ShellBags, MTP/PTP awareness for smartphones/cameras already in catalog. Implemented: enriched evtx_driver_frameworks meaning text with disabled-by-default warning + wevtutil enable command + fallback pivot chain + MTP/PTP gap note; added Carvey 2026 + Carvey 2022 USB redux + ElComSoft 2026 sources. No heuristic threshold extractable from this post (qualitative guidance only — "understand your goals", "maintain playbooks" are not encodable predicates). 1 artifact enriched / 0 new artifacts / 0 heuristics.

title: Views on AI & the Anthropic Report
notes: re-reviewed under full standard (artifacts + heuristics): pure editorial/opinion on the Anthropic "Disrupting the first reported AI-orchestrated cyber espionage campaign" PDF. Carvey's central thesis is explicit: "there was nothing obvious shared in the Anthropic report that says any of the tools or techniques used by the AI was any different from what a human would do, only that it was faster." Post offers zero artifact paths, zero registry keys, zero event IDs, zero file layouts, zero field offsets, zero decoder formats, zero MITRE technique mappings beyond what's already in the catalog from human-operated ransomware coverage. Quantitative claims ("80-90% of tactical operations", "30 organizations", "physically impossible request rates") are summary statistics from the source report, not encodable detection thresholds — no requests/second number, no time-window, no per-host cap, no specific telemetry source bound to the rate. The "OODA loop tighter, artifacts generated closer together" observation is a qualitative timeline-density signal but Carvey provides no boundary (events/min, dwell-time delta, parallel-session count) that maps to a heuristic predicate. Post also recycles the same human-operated-ransomware playbook reference (Microsoft 2020) already cited by other catalog entries. 0 artifacts / 0 heuristics — no encodable content in THIS post.

title: Questions I've Been Asked
notes: re-reviewed under full standard (artifacts + heuristics): pure methodology Q&A. Carvey answers reader/webinar questions about his triage approach ("I cheat" — uses RegRipper plugins enriched from prior engagements + Yara-via-RegRipper, rather than parsing the entire Registry by hand), whether his methodology changes for DC vs workstation ("for the most part, it doesn't"), why he won't write a Linux forensics book, and where to find free forensic images from real incidents. Post merely *references* his other 2026/01 posts ("grab-bag" Valley RAT registry path, already implemented as valley_rat_registry; the 2023 Yara-via-RegRipper post) without restating any path/key/value/GUID/offset/field/event-ID. Zero new registry paths, zero file paths, zero binary layouts, zero decoder specs, zero MITRE techniques, zero detection thresholds, zero behavioral signals with quantitative boundaries. The "enrich parser output with notes from prior engagements" workflow is a general DFIR practice, not an encodable predicate. 0 artifacts / 0 heuristics — no encodable content in THIS post.

title: Human Behavior In Digital Forensics, pt II
notes: Carvey case studies on human behaviour in DFIR: (1) Targeted Threat Actor — RAT installer pushed to 8 endpoints via Scheduled Task, second variant dropped in StartUp folder of communal admin account, recovered original installer from VSCs; (2) Disgruntled Employee — Thursday 9pm console login, browser drive-by download, persistence in HKCU Run key, ransomware launched on Friday morning login, timeline reconstructed via Registry LastWrite + Event Log + file system metadata + browser history; (3) RAT Removal — Windows 7 laptop via VPN from China, "all parts of the buffalo" (file system, WEVTX, Registry, VSCs, hibernation file) showed user attached USB and ran installer, then made manual cleanup attempts; (4) Non-PCI Breach — keylogger on home system, RDP into healthcare org, fresh user profile creation gave timeline visibility, threat actor searched "password" and misspellings of "banking" indicating opportunistic/unprepared; all artifact categories already in catalog (run_key_hkcu, startup_folder_user, scheduled_tasks_dir, evtx_security, vsc, etc.); no specific new key paths, GUIDs, event IDs, or field schemas to implement re-reviewed 2026-05-10: confirmed

title: Investigating Time Stomping
notes: implemented: enable_periodic_backup (HKLM\SYSTEM\CCS\Control\Session Manager\Configuration Manager\EnablePeriodicBackup REG_DWORD, T1070.006) — Carvey EndNote recommends enabling this Win10 1803+ default-disabled value to restore RegBack 10-day periodic hive backups, providing live-vs-RegBack LastWrite comparison to detect registry time stomping. Cross-correlates with Microsoft-Windows-Shell-Core/Operational.evtx Run/RunOnce processed events (already in catalog). NTFS $SI/$FN time stomping (T1070.006) already covered conceptually by existing $MFT and $UsnJrnl artifacts (fa_file_environ_systemdrive_mft, fa_file_extend_usnjrnl, kape_file_extend_usnjrnl_j) — Carvey's post defers to Lina Lau's blog and the SANS DFIR Summit 2023 talk for the canonical $SI/$FN detection methodology, which is well-documented in existing parsers (MFTECmd, AnalyzeMFT). notatin (StrozFriedberg Rust registry parser) is a tool, not an artifact. re-reviewed 2026-05-10: confirmed

title: The State of Windows Digital Analysis, pt II
notes: implemented: evtx_application_msiinstaller (Application.evtx Source=MsiInstaller, Event IDs 1022/1033/1034/1035/1036/1040/1042/11707/11708/11724, T1218.007/T1204.002/T1091, High). Carvey's central concrete artifact gap — open Raspberry Robin reports (Red Canary, Cybereason, Microsoft) all omit MsiInstaller records even though every msiexec /i writes them. Sources: Huntress Raspberry Robin deconstruction, MS MSI event-logging docs, Carvey post itself. The post is otherwise meta-commentary on open-reporting gaps; RunOnce (already cataloged: run_key_hkcu_once / run_key_hklm_once) is the only other concrete Carvey-cited artifact and is already covered. MFT / Windows Event Log / Registry generic categories already covered by existing fa_file_environ_systemdrive_mft, evtx_*, and registry hive descriptors. re-reviewed 2026-05-10: confirmed

title: The State of Windows Digital Analysis
notes: no new artifacts: Carvey methodology/opinion piece criticizing widespread misinterpretation of ShimCache (file system $STANDARD_INFORMATION last-modification timestamps from MFT, NOT execution time — easily time-stomped) and AmCache ("the user's Amcache.hve" is a fiction, citing dfir.ru CIT/Syscache and ANSSI testing) in open DFIR blogs. The only concrete artifact-level guidance — MsiInstaller records (Event IDs 1040/1042) in Application Event Log corroborating msiexec executions per Huntress Raspberry Robin — is already cataloged as evtx_application_msiinstaller (added during the Pt II review on line 262). ShimCache/AmCache themselves are general categories whose underlying registry keys (AppCompatCache, Amcache.hve InventoryApplicationFile) and time-stomping detection ($MFT $SI/$FN comparison) are already covered by existing descriptors (fa_file_environ_systemdrive_mft, AppCompatCache/AmCache entries in windows_registry_ext*). No new registry keys, file paths, event IDs, GUIDs, or field schemas introduced by this post. re-reviewed 2026-05-10: confirmed

title: Book Review: Effective Threat Investigation for SOC Analysts
notes: no new artifacts: Carvey's review of Mostafa Yahia's SOC analyst book. Reinforces existing tradecraft: do not view artifacts in isolation, build artifact constellations, develop timelines from start of investigation, audit policy gaps mean Security.evtx may lack expected logon events. The four log channels Carvey enumerates as a logon constellation — Security.evtx (4624/4625/4634/4647/4648/4672), Microsoft-Windows-TerminalServices-LocalSessionManager/Operational (21/22/23/24/25), Microsoft-Windows-User Profile Service/Operational (1/2/4), Microsoft-Windows-Shell-Core/Operational, Microsoft-Windows-TaskScheduler/Operational (106/140/141/200/201) — are all already cataloged across windows_evtx_ext.rs and the evtx_generated.rs feed. No new registry keys, file paths, event IDs, GUIDs, or field schemas introduced. re-reviewed 2026-05-10: confirmed
